Beijing often frames tensions in the South China Sea as a reaction to US military presence in the Philippines.

But the timeline doesn’t support that claim. Chinese expansion continued even after US bases closed in the early 1990s.

In 2016, Permanent Court of Arbitration ruled overwhelmingly in favor of Manila, rejecting China’s sweeping maritime claims.

Beijing rejected the ruling and continued its activities.
In the Philippines’ waters, militia boats have been accused of sideswiping patrol vessels, damaging them and risking lives while China denies direct military intent.
